items.name: S&P 500 ETF
items.note: still digesting Alphabet's and Tesla's capex-driven pullback, with the July 28-29 Fed meeting the next big catalyst

items.name: Crude oil (WTI)
items.note: holding near six-week highs as Mideast shipping risk keeps a floor under crude

items.name: Gold (per oz)
items.note: easing back below $4,100 as haven demand cools slightly even with the Fed's hawkish tilt still in place

items.name: Nvidia

items.name: Microsoft

items.name: Alphabet
items.note: fell after raising 2026 capex guidance to $205B despite a Q2 cloud-revenue beat (+82% to $24.8B), as investors want clearer proof AI spending converts to profit

items.name: Oracle
items.note: still working through the same AI-capex-return debate weighing on peers this earnings season

items.name: Advanced Micro Devices
items.note: still riding this week's 'Advancing AI 2026' event momentum (Zen 6 Venice CPU, MI400-series roadmap)

items.name: Taiwan Semiconductor

items.name: Tesla (Optimus)
items.note: Q2 results missed estimates and 2026 capex guidance rose to $25B for AI infrastructure, Dojo 2, robotaxi and Optimus, with shares weakening post-earnings as OpenAI's new robotics division adds competitive pressure

items.name: Intuitive Surgical
items.note: still working through a cautious post-earnings guide on procedure growth

items.name: Teradyne

items.name: UBTECH Robotics
items.note: quote is a few sessions old; humanoid-robotics supply-chain names elsewhere (Ouster, Vishay Precision Group) are surging on Agility Robotics' Nasdaq SPAC debut

items.name: ServiceNow
items.note: beat Q2 estimates and raised subscription guidance, but shares fell anyway as investors rotate away from richly valued AI software; price now reflects last December's 5-for-1 split, correcting a stale prior quote

items.name: Cadence Design Systems

items.name: Synopsys
items.note: still working off last week's EDA-disruption sell-off, even as some analysts call the reaction overblown

items.name: Bitcoin

items.name: Ethereum
items.note: roughly flat, holding its recent gains as risk appetite stays intact

items.name: CrowdStrike
items.note: named among Morgan Stanley's highest-conviction Overweight picks in the same note that downgraded Adobe and Salesforce

items.name: Zscaler
items.note: still below its tracked entry, even as cybersecurity names keep outperforming broader enterprise software

items.name: ServiceNow
items.note: beat Q2 estimates and raised guidance but fell anyway; price now reflects last December's 5-for-1 split, correcting a stale prior quote

outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: Asian chipmakers rallied broadly (Kospi +2.8%) as Alphabet's plan to spend up to $200B this year on AI computing power reassured markets that AI infrastructure demand isn't slowing — Samsung and SK Hynix each gained over 3%, and Nvidia extended its own rebound.

The Amplify Cybersecurity ETF (HACK) is up 15.4% over the past 30 days and hit a fresh 12-month high in early July; Morgan Stanley reinforced the sector's relative strength today by naming CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks among its highest-conviction Overweight picks in the same note that downgraded Adobe and Salesforce.

weakest.reason: Morgan Stanley downgraded Adobe to Underweight (price target cut to $240 from $365) and Salesforce to Equalweight (target cut to $185 from $287), citing AI-transition risk and, for Salesforce, Agentforce adoption that hasn't yet moved the needle on subscription revenue growth; ServiceNow shares fell today despite beating Q2 estimates.

Alphabet and Tesla both reported real business strength (Alphabet's cloud revenue up 82%; Tesla raising 2026 capex) but shares in both fell on the size of their new spending guidance ($205B and $25B respectively), as investors push back on the AI-investment cycle until there's clearer proof it converts to profit.

outlook.nextStep: The chip-sector rebound and cybersecurity's relative strength look like the more durable, multi-day trends worth a closer look; the Adobe/Salesforce downgrades and the Cadence/Synopsys EDA sell-off are sharper, single-catalyst moves better watched for confirmation before drawing conclusions, especially with the July 28-29 Fed meeting still ahead.

signals.title: Fed expected to hold steady July 28-29, but inflation and energy costs keep the door open to a hike
signals.body: The federal funds rate has sat at 3.50%-3.75% since June under new Chair Kevin Warsh, and market-implied odds put an 79.5% chance the Fed holds again at its July 28-29 meeting. May's CPI print showed a 4.2% year-over-year rise, driven partly by a 23.5% surge in energy costs tied to Middle East tensions, which has kept traders wary of a later hike.
signals.watch: Any fresh inflation data ahead of the July 28-29 meeting, and whether Fed commentary shifts toward openness to tightening.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — a binary policy event is still a week away, with inflation data that could move it either direction.

signals.title: Alphabet and Tesla both beat on parts of their business but sold off on bigger capex guidance
signals.body: Alphabet's Q2 cloud revenue rose 82% to $24.8B and consolidated revenue grew 24%, but shares fell as management raised 2026 capital-spending guidance to as much as $205B from $190B. Tesla's Q2 results missed estimates, and it raised its own 2026 capex guidance to $25B from $20B for AI infrastructure, Dojo 2, robotaxi manufacturing and Optimus — with OpenAI's newly announced robotics division adding fresh competitive pressure to the Optimus narrative.
signals.watch: Whether other AI-infrastructure names report similar capex increases this earnings season, and how the market treats spending-versus-returns questions going forward.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — strong underlying metrics are being overshadowed by investor skepticism about the size and payoff of AI capex.

signals.title: Cadence and Synopsys bouncing back from an AI chip-design scare
signals.body: Both stocks sold off 8-10% last Friday after Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 model reportedly designed a functional chip in 48 hours using only open-source EDA tools, raising fears of disruption to the EDA industry. Mizuho and BNP both called the reaction overblown, with Mizuho arguing the model orchestrated existing open-source tools rather than replacing deterministic, physically accurate EDA software. Cadence is recovering more today than Synopsys.
signals.watch: Whether the 'buy the dip' analyst view holds up, or whether more AI-designed-chip demonstrations keep pressuring EDA valuations.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a concrete technical debate is playing out in real time with an observable, divergent price reaction across the two stocks.

signals.title: Morgan Stanley draws a sharp line within enterprise software
signals.body: The firm downgraded Adobe to Underweight (price target cut to $240 from $365) and Salesforce to Equalweight (target cut to $185 from $287), citing AI-transition risk, a freemium pivot and leadership change at Adobe, and Agentforce adoption at Salesforce that hasn't yet accelerated subscription revenue. In the same note, it named Microsoft, ServiceNow, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, Cloudflare, Datadog and Snowflake as its highest-conviction Overweight picks — even as ServiceNow's own shares fell today despite beating Q2 estimates.
signals.watch: Whether other analysts follow with similar downgrades of legacy software names, and whether the winners Morgan Stanley named continue to separate from the group.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — this is a specific, well-reasoned thesis about winners and losers within one theme rather than a broad sector call.

signals.title: Humanoid-robotics supply chain keeps running hot on Agility Robotics' Nasdaq debut
signals.body: Agility Robotics' $2.5B SPAC merger with Churchill Capital Corp XI (expected to trade as AGLT) has driven big moves in supply-chain names: Vishay Precision Group is up roughly 269% year-to-date after booking over $37M in FY2025 humanoid orders, and Ouster is up about 150% year-to-date. This comes even as Tesla, the highest-profile humanoid name, saw shares weaken post-earnings on Optimus timeline questions.
signals.watch: Whether Agility Robotics' listing formally closes, and whether other humanoid-adjacent suppliers see similar order flow.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a new IPO is reshaping how the market prices this supply chain, somewhat independent of Tesla-specific execution risk.

signals.title: Bitcoin consolidates after its best month since January as ETF inflows resume
signals.body: Bitcoin is holding in a $64,000-$66,800 range for a third straight session after rallying more than 13% off its July 1 low of $57,750. Spot Bitcoin ETFs posted positive net inflows for a second consecutive week — the first time that's happened since May — while TRON's addition to the S&P Pantera Digital Asset Index points to continued institutional benchmarking of the sector.
signals.watch: Whether the ETF inflow streak extends to a third week, and whether Bitcoin breaks out of its current range in either direction.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — the multi-week inflow trend is a steadier data point than day-to-day price consolidation.

signals.title: Cybersecurity's outperformance gets a fresh vote of confidence
signals.body: The Amplify Cybersecurity ETF (HACK) is up 15.4% over the past 30 days and hit a 12-month high in early July. Today's Morgan Stanley note reinforced that strength, naming CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks among its highest-conviction Overweight picks in the same report that downgraded Adobe and Salesforce — underscoring the widening gap between security spending and general enterprise software budgets.
signals.watch: Upcoming cybersecurity earnings for confirmation of continued budget resilience, and whether premium valuations in the group hold if the broader market pulls back.
signals.focus: Worth checking in on, but keep in mind some of this strength may already be priced into current valuations.
